Studio

Urban spaces where
people & nature meet

I am a landscape architect graduated from the Swedish University of Agricultural Sciences (SLU), with a particular interest in how urban spaces can be both expressive and inclusive — places where people, animals, and nature have room to interact and shape their surroundings.

Born in Paris, raised in Junsele in Ångermanland, and ultimately in Stockholm. An exchange year at ENSP Marseille sharpened my interest in Mediterranean urbanism, temporality, and the right to public space.

Through hands-on work in landscape maintenance — at Bromma Cemetery, Funkia, and gardens in Marseille — I have developed an understanding of what it takes for vegetation to thrive in urban environments: the relationship between idea, execution, and long-term management.

Alchemilla

A fabricated superplant capable of harvesting atmospheric water — a future response to urban water scarcity.

How residents' own expressions can be integrated into contemporary urban planning through temporary landscape architecture.
